title: Own-port server + responsive phone-first board UI

tests.summary: 17 passed (board view, per-card-ref resolver, path confinement incl. symlink, card-id + sliceId validation)

e2e.summary: ALL assertions pass: full pipeline renders, responsive at 390px + 320px, card detail shows the decision-10 links, populated card shows a video LINK (FINDING 8/10), Adv-Review Watch surfaces the CODEX CALL line (FINDING 6), parked card in needs-attention (FINDING 3), no console errors

codexReview.notes: 6 security fixes (arbitrary read → per-card refs, symlink → realpath, card-id + sliceId traversal, tampered-id write, project-global evidenceIndex)

codexPwTest.summary: Codex sandbox CANNOT launch Chromium (MachPortRendezvousServer Permission denied 1100) — an environment limitation of the codex sandbox, NOT a board defect. Functional verification done by the same-runtime committed Playwright e2e pass (all assertions green); the cross-model REVIEW (3A, approve r6) is the second-model opinion.

by: vision review of the recorded screenshots
designAudit.notes: matches the v4 wireframe + the Garrison shell visual language: accent-blue agent lists, codex-purple adversarial lists, dashed interactive Discuss, card chips (project/iter/goalMode), correct button set (Advance on manual / Start on agent), mobile bottom-sheet card detail at 390px. No emoji in UI.

video.notes: recorded webm + gif + 6 screenshots (desktop board, card detail, phone 390/320, populated-card video link, Adv-Review CODEX CALL)

blockers.cause: codex sandbox cannot launch a browser (MachPortRendezvousServer Permission denied 1100)
blockers.remediationAttempted: ran `codex exec -s workspace-write -c sandbox_workspace_write.network_access=true` driving playwright; Chromium failed to start in the codex sandbox (not a board defect). Functional pass done via the repo's own headless Playwright outside the codex sandbox.
